Generative Augmented Flow Networks Ling Pan1∗ Dinghuai Zhang1 Aaron Courville1 Longbo Huang2 Yoshua Bengio1 1Mila, Université de Montréal 2 Tsinghua University Abstract The Generative Flow Network (Bengio et al., 2021b, GFlowNet) is a probabilistic framework where an agent learns a stochastic policy for object generati…

Yet, GFlowNets only learn from the reward of the terminal state, and do not consider interme- diate rewards, which can limit its applicability, especially in more general RL settings. Rewards play a critical role in learning (Silver et al., 2021). The tremendous success of RL largely depends on the reward signals that …

and state-action pairs correspond to edges. The goal for GFlowNets is to learn a stochastic policy π that can construct discrete objects x∈X with probability proportional to the reward function R :X →R≥0, i.e.,π(x)∝ R(x). GFlowNets construct objects sequentially, where each step adds an element to the construction. We …
